title: wedelia montevidensis
intro: Wedelia montevidensis, also known as the Sumpa or Butterfly Weed, is a flowering plant native to South America that belongs to the daisy family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Wedelia montevidensis include reducing inflammation and pain, treating fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as being used topically to apply its medicinal properties to the skin.
